title: The image
why.empty: The editor is empty. You deleted the listing instead of adding a photo. Bring the code back.
why.not-closed: Some other tag is open and never closed. ( does not count — it has no closing twin.)
why.no-img: There is no image. The tag is .
hint2.no-img: Put it where you say "Here is a photo of it" — right after that paragraph.
why.no-src: The tag is there, but it does not say which image — or the src is empty. With no address the browser does not know what to show and leaves a hole.
hint2.no-src: src is an attribute. It lives inside the tag, in quotes.
hint3.no-src: An empty src="" is the same as none at all. It points nowhere.
why.closed-img: You wrote . There is no such thing. wraps nothing — it simply places an image on the spot. A closing tag would be pointless.
hint2.closed-img:
Remember
. It has no closing twin either.
hint3.closed-img: A closing tag only makes sense when something is being wrapped. What would the image be wrapping?
hint4.closed-img: Delete the </img>. The tag ends where it ends — with a >.
why.wrong-path: The path does not point at the image. It is at /uroci/bike.jpg.
hint2.wrong-path: Check for a typo. And remember: capital letters matter on a server.
hint3.wrong-path: Bike.JPG and bike.jpg are different files where the site lives. On your machine — they are not. That is where "it works on mine" comes from.
why.lost-price: The price is gone. The task is to add an image, not to delete things.
blocks.0.text: The listing needs a photo. Otherwise nobody will look at it.
blocks.3.text: src comes from "source". It says which file to show.
blocks.4.text:
Notice: there is no . There is nothing to wrap — it holds no text, it places something on the spot. Like
and , which you already know.
blocks.6.text: Everything from the paths lesson applies here. /uroci/bike.jpg. If the image is in a folder next to you — images/bike.jpg. If it is on someone else's site — the full address.
blocks.7.text: And the same trap: a path from your own computer works only on your computer.
blocks.8.text: And capital letters matter. Bike.JPG and bike.jpg are different files on a server.
blocks.10.text: Run it and look. The image comes out at its true size — it might be 3000 pixels wide and run off the screen.
blocks.11.text: You will want to shrink it. There are width and height attributes and they work. But do not use them for looks — size is CSS.
blocks.12.text: The tag says "here is an image". How big it appears is decided by the style.
blocks.13.text: There is one good reason to add them, though: if you say in advance how much room the image will take, the browser reserves it — and the page does not jump around while it loads. Everyone has cursed a site where the text ran away just before they clicked.
blocks.15.text: The image is there and it shows. But it is mute — it says nothing about itself. If it fails to load, there will be a hole where it stood. The next lesson is about that.
